entry.title: Re: Why are you not utilizing Built-In Support feature in SAP Cloud ALM?
entry.content: Very insightful post—Built-In Support in SAP Cloud ALM is clearly underutilized despite its strong AI-driven capabilities like context-aware recommendations and Incident Solution Matching. Leveraging these features can significantly reduce resolution time and improve self-service efficiency. Alongside this, integrating strong data strategies with solutions like Cloud Data Management for SAP can further enhance system performance, streamline support processes, and ensure better governance in complex SAP landscapes.

entry.title: Re: Public Cloud – Standard CDS for SII eDocument status/...
entry.content:
Good day @Veronica_VidalTorres 

Thank you for your question.

I’m not aware of a standard released CDS view that specifically exposes Spanish SII eDocument submission in AEAT together with statuses like CREA, REGIS, RERR, or REJE for reporting. As you mentioned, you can manage these in Manage Electronic Documents or eDocument Cockpit.

If you are considering integration API for Electronic Documents is an option that likely supports the path than a CDS for your use case.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Partner Learning Accelerator: SAP Business Data Cloud Express Academy
entry.content:
If you’re an SAP Partner who keeps hearing “SAP Business Data Cloud” in every strategy meeting and thinks, I really need to get ahead of this, this academy is for you.

Our Partner learning accelerator: Certified academy for SAP Business Data Cloud brings SAP experts and partners together to demystify:

  • SAP Analytics Cloud
  • SAP Datasphere
  • SAP Business Data Cloud

We’ll walk through real-world flows, not just slides, how data moves, how models are built, how insights land in the hands of business users.  Plus, you’ll prove your skills with a hands-on test in a live system before you even book the certification exam.

The goal?  You walk away with the fundamentals and core concepts to feel genuinely ready for the SAP Certified - SAP Business Data Cloud exam.  You just need basic data & analytics knowledge and the willingness to lean in and learn.

Honestly, if you want to be “the cloud data go-to” in your team, this is a smart place to start.  Register now.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2025/may/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00010285.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #BusinessDataCloud #SAPSkills #DataAndAnalytics #SAPTraining

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps Deprecation and The Path Forward
entry.content: Shocking to hear. SAP Build II, shares the same fate as SAP Build I.  Considering SAP Build Community edition's title was "AppGyver is free, forever" and let non-SAP people keep building apps there, then take it away from them in a few years, will make them think SAP in not trustable. But is it only the nonSAP people that will feel betrayed? SAP Sales and Service Cloud Version 2 has been coupled with SAP Build apps and for the last few years all we hear is SAP Build Apps is a must learn for IT. Consulting firms advertise it to customers, helding sessions spending time on training etc. Then we hear these news. Simply disappointing.

entry.title: KPI (Key Performance Indicator): A Simple Guide
entry.content:
Introduction:  A Key Performance Indicator (KPI) is a measurable value used to evaluate how effectively a business or organization is achieving its key objectives. In SAP Fiori Elements, KPIs are used to display important business metrics in a clear and visual format. They help users quickly monitor performance, identify trends, and make data-driven decisions.

KPI (Key Performance Indicator):  It is a measurable value used to evaluate how effectively a business or organization is achieving its objectives. KPIs help businesses monitor performance, identify trends, and support data-driven decision-making.

In modern enterprise applications built with SAP Fiori Elements, KPIs provide a quick overview of key business metrics directly within dashboards and analytical applications.

KPI in Analytical List Page:

The Analytical List Page (ALP) is a powerful Fiori Elements template that combines:

  • KPIs
  • Charts
  • Filters
  • Tables

KPIs are displayed at the Top of the page and help users quickly analyze business data.

Note: In this article, I have not covered the creation of the CDS View, OData service exposure, or the application creation in SAP BTP, as these topics were already explained in my previous blogs. In this post, I focus only on providing an example of the KPI annotations and explaining where these annotations are added in the project.

CDS:

You can add the KPI annotations in the Metadata Extension file. This approach helps keep UI-related annotations separate from the core CDS view and allows developers to manage UI configurations more efficiently.

KPI Annotation:

.chart: [{
    qualifier: 'chartDefault',
    description: 'Purchase Data',
    chartType: #PIE,
    dimensions: ['Month_Year'],
    measures: ['Amount'],
    dimensionAttributes: [{ dimension: 'Month_Year',role: #SERIES }],
    measureAttributes: [{ measure: 'Amount', role: #AXIS_1 }]
    },
    {
    qualifier: 'kpidata',
    description: 'Purchase Data',
    chartType: #DONUT,
    dimensions: ['Month_Year'],
    measures: ['Amount'],
    dimensionAttributes: [{ dimension: 'Month_Year',role: #SERIES }],
    measureAttributes: [{ measure: 'Amount', role: #AXIS_1 }]
    }

   ]

@UI.presentationVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'Default',
    visualizations: [{ type: #AS_CHART ,qualifier: 'chartDefault'}]
    },
 {
 qualifier: 'KPIAMT',text: 'KPI: Amount by Year',
 visualizations: [{ type: #AS_CHART,qualifier: 'kpidata'},{ type: #AS_DATAPOINT,qualifier: 'Amount'
}]

}]

@UI.selectionVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    text: 'Amount by Year'
}]
@UI.selectionPresentationVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    presentationVariantQualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    selectionVariantQualifier: 'KPIAMT'
}]

Once the annotations are added in the Metadata Extension, you can configure the KPI annotation in the manifest.json file so that it is recognized and displayed in the Analytical List Page application.

qualifier: 'KPIAMT'
 

"keyPerformanceIndicators": {
                            "kpiNayak": {
                                "model": "KPI1",
                                "entitySet": "Ztest_ALP_Report",
                                "qualifier": "KPIAMT"
                            }
                        }

Path:

sap.ui.generic.app → pages → AnalyticalListPage → component → settings → keyPerformanceIndicators
 "pages": {
            "AnalyticalListPage|Ztest_ALP_Report": {
                "entitySet": "Ztest_ALP_Report",
                "component": {
                    "name": "sap.suite.ui.generic.template.AnalyticalListPage",
                    "list": true,
                    "settings": {
                        "condensedTableLayout": true,
                        "showGoButtonOnFilterBar": true,
                        "qualifier": "Default",
                        "autoHide": true,
                        "smartVariantManagement": false,
                        "tableSettings": {
                            "multiSelect": false,
                            "type": "AnalyticalTable"
                        },
                        "keyPerformanceIndicators": {
                            "kpiNayak": {
                                "model": "KPI1",
                                "entitySet": "Ztest_ALP_Report",
                                "qualifier": "KPIAMT"
                            }
                        }
                    }
                },

Note : After that, ensure that the model used for the KPI configuration is also created under the models section in the manifest.json file. The same model name defined for the KPI must be maintained in the models configuration so that the application can correctly retrieve the KPI data from the OData service.

The model name used in the KPI configuration (for example, KPI1) must also be created under the models section in the manifest.json file. The same model name should be maintained .

Path:

sap.ui5 → models → KPI1

    "KPI1": {
                "dataSource": "mainService",
                "preload": true,
                "settings": {
                    "defaultBindingMode": "TwoWay",
                    "defaultCountMode": "Inline",
                    "refreshAfterChange": false
                }
            },
"models": {
            "i18n": {
                "type": "sap.ui.model.resource.ResourceModel",
                "settings": {
                    "bundleName": "analyticalreport.i18n.i18n"
                }
            },
            "": {
                "dataSource": "mainService",
                "preload": true,
                "settings": {
                    "defaultBindingMode": "TwoWay",
                    "defaultCountMode": "Inline",
                    "refreshAfterChange": false,
                    "metadataUrlParams": {
                        "sap-value-list": "none"
                    }
                }
            },
            "KPI1": {
                "dataSource": "mainService",
                "preload": true,
                "settings": {
                    "defaultBindingMode": "TwoWay",
                    "defaultCountMode": "Inline",
                    "refreshAfterChange": false
                }
            },

Once all the configurations are completed, the KPI is successfully created. You can see the KPI displayed in the Analytical List Page as shown in the screenshot below for your reference.

Full Codes: This code example covers the Analytical List Page, including the configuration of Visual Filters and KPI annotations used in the application.

1.CDS View:
-------------
@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZALPR'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'ALP Report'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
define view Ztest_ALP_Report
  as select from ztest_sales_tab
{
  key vbeln as SalesDocument,
      vkorg as SalesOrg,
      werks as Plant,
      vtweg as DistrChannel,
      @DefaultAggregation: #SUM
      netwr as Amount,
      waerk as Currency,
      audat as D_Date,
      case substring( audat, 5, 2 )
      when '01' then concat( 'JAN-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '02' then concat( 'FEB-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '03' then concat( 'MAR-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '04' then concat( 'APR-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '05' then concat( 'MAY-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '06' then concat( 'JUN-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '07' then concat( 'JUL-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '08' then concat( 'AUG-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '09' then concat( 'SEP-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '10' then concat( 'OCT-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '11' then concat( 'NOV-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '12' then concat( 'DEC-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )

      end   as Month_Year
}
___________________________________
2.Meta data Extension:
------------------------
@Metadata.layer: #CORE

@UI.chart: [{
    qualifier: 'chartDefault',
    description: 'Purchase Data',
    chartType: #PIE,
    dimensions: ['Month_Year'],
    measures: ['Amount'],
    dimensionAttributes: [{ dimension: 'Month_Year',role: #SERIES }],
    measureAttributes: [{ measure: 'Amount', role: #AXIS_1 }]
    },
    {
    qualifier: 'kpidata',
    description: 'Purchase Data',
    chartType: #DONUT,
    dimensions: ['Month_Year'],
    measures: ['Amount'],
    dimensionAttributes: [{ dimension: 'Month_Year',role: #SERIES }],
    measureAttributes: [{ measure: 'Amount', role: #AXIS_1 }]
    }

   ]

@UI.presentationVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'Default',
    visualizations: [{ type: #AS_CHART ,qualifier: 'chartDefault'}]
    },
 {
 qualifier: 'KPIAMT',text: 'KPI: Amount by Year',
 visualizations: [{ type: #AS_CHART,qualifier: 'kpidata'},{ type: #AS_DATAPOINT,qualifier: 'Amount'
}]

}]

@UI.selectionVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    text: 'Amount by Year'
}]
@UI.selectionPresentationVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    presentationVariantQualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    selectionVariantQualifier: 'KPIAMT'
}]


annotate entity Ztest_ALP_Report with
{
  .lineItem: [{ position: 10 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 10 }]

  SalesDocument;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 20 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 20 }]
  SalesOrg;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 30 }]
  Plant;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 40 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 40 }]
  DistrChannel;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 50 }]
  .dataPoint.title: 'Total Amount'
  .dataPoint.qualifier: 'Amount'
  Amount;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 60 }]
  Currency;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 70 }]
  D_Date;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 80 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 80 }]
  Month_Year;

}
__________________________________
3. Annotation.xml file:
-----------------------------
<edmx:Edmx xmlns:edmx="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edmx" Version="4.0">
    <edmx:Reference Uri="https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/Common.xml">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.Common.v1" Alias="Common"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/UI.xml">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1" Alias="UI"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_SRV/$metadata">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_SRV" Alias="SAP"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:DataServices>
        <Schema xmlns="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edm" Namespace="local">
           
            <Annotations Target="SAP.Ztest_ALP_ReportType">
                <Annotation Term="UI.Chart" Qualifier="Step1_CQ">
                    <Record Type="UI.ChartDefinitionType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="ChartType" EnumMember="UI.ChartType/Bar"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Title" String="Purchase Order Data"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Description" String="Purchase Order Data"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Dimensions">
                            <Collection>
                                <PropertyPath>Month_Year</PropertyPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="DimensionAttributes">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="UI.ChartDimensionAttributeType">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Dimension" PropertyPath="Month_Year"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Role" EnumMember="UI.ChartDimensionRoleType/Category"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Measures">
                            <Collection>
                                <PropertyPath>Amount</PropertyPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="MeasureAttributes">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="UI.ChartMeasureAttributeType">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Measure" PropertyPath="Amount"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Role" EnumMember="UI.ChartMeasureRoleType/Axis1"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
                <Annotation Term="UI.PresentationVariant" Qualifier="Step2_pQ">
                    <Record Type="UI.PresentationVariantType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="Visualizations">
                            <Collection>
                                <AnnotationPath>@UI.Chart#Step1_CQ</AnnotationPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
            </Annotations>
            <Annotations Target="SAP.Ztest_ALP_ReportType/Month_Year">
                <Annotation Term="Common.ValueList">
                    <Record Type="Common.ValueListType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="CollectionPath" String="Ztest_ALP_Report"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="PresentationVariantQualifier" String="Step2_pQ"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Parameters">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="Common.ValueListParameterInOut">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="LocalDataProperty" PropertyPath="Month_Year"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="ValueListProperty" String="Month_Year"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
            </Annotations>
           
           
        </Schema>
    </edmx:DataServices>
</edmx:Edmx>
 _____________________________          
4. Manifest.json File:
----------------------------------------------
{
    "_version": "1.82.0",
    "sap.app": {
        "id": "analyticalreport",
        "type": "application",
        "i18n": "i18n/i18n.properties",
        "applicationVersion": {
            "version": "0.0.1"
        },
        "title": "{{appTitle}}",
        "description": "{{appDescription}}",
        "resources": "resources.json",
        "sourceTemplate": {
            "id": "@sap/generator-fiori:alp",
            "version": "1.20.4",
            "toolsId": "530787e5-fdb9-4d9d-87bf-ca33db19cf1d"
        },
        "dataSources": {
            "ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_ANNO_MDL": {
                "uri": "/sap/opu/odata/IWFND/CATALOGSERVICE;v=2/Annotations(TechnicalName='ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_ANNO_MDL',Version='0001')/$value/",
                "type": "ODataAnnotation",
                "settings": {
                    "localUri": "localService/mainService/ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_ANNO_MDL.xml"
                }
            },
            "annotation": {
                "type": "ODataAnnotation",
                "uri": "annotations/annotation.xml",
                "settings": {
                    "localUri": "annotations/annotation.xml"
                }
            },
            "mainService": {
                "uri": "/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_SRV/",
                "type": "OData",
                "settings": {
                    "annotations": [
                        "ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_ANNO_MDL",
                        "annotation"
                    ],
                    "localUri": "localService/mainService/metadata.xml",
                    "odataVersion": "2.0"
                }
            }
        }
    },
    "sap.ui": {
        "technology": "UI5",
        "icons": {
            "icon": "",
            "favIcon": "",
            "phone": "",
            "phone@2": "",
            "tablet": "",
            "tablet@2": ""
        },
        "deviceTypes": {
            "desktop": true,
            "tablet": true,
            "phone": true
        }
    },
    "sap.ui5": {
        "flexEnabled": true,
        "dependencies": {
            "minUI5Version": "1.145.0",
            "libs": {
                "sap.m": {},
                "sap.ui.core": {},
                "sap.ushell": {},
                "sap.f": {},
                "sap.ui.comp": {},
                "sap.ui.generic.app": {},
                "sap.suite.ui.generic.template": {}
            }
        },
        "contentDensities": {
            "compact": true,
            "cozy": true
        },
        "models": {
            "i18n": {
                "type": "sap.ui.model.resource.ResourceModel",
                "settings": {
                    "bundleName": "analyticalreport.i18n.i18n"
                }
            },
            "": {
                "dataSource": "mainService",
                "preload": true,
                "settings": {
                    "defaultBindingMode": "TwoWay",
                    "defaultCountMode": "Inline",
                    "refreshAfterChange": false,
                    "metadataUrlParams": {
                        "sap-value-list": "none"
                    }
                }
            },
            "KPI1": {
                "dataSource": "mainService",
                "preload": true,
                "settings": {
                    "defaultBindingMode": "TwoWay",
                    "defaultCountMode": "Inline",
                    "refreshAfterChange": false
                }
            },
            "@i18n": {
                "type": "sap.ui.model.resource.ResourceModel",
                "uri": "i18n/i18n.properties"
            }
        },
        "resources": {
            "css": []
        },
        "routing": {
            "config": {},
            "routes": [],
            "targets": {}
        }
    },
    "sap.ui.generic.app": {
        "_version": "1.3.0",
        "settings": {
            "forceGlobalRefresh": false,
            "objectPageHeaderType": "Dynamic",
            "considerAnalyticalParameters": true,
            "showDraftToggle": false
        },
        "pages": {
            "AnalyticalListPage|Ztest_ALP_Report": {
                "entitySet": "Ztest_ALP_Report",
                "component": {
                    "name": "sap.suite.ui.generic.template.AnalyticalListPage",
                    "list": true,
                    "settings": {
                        "condensedTableLayout": true,
                        "showGoButtonOnFilterBar": true,
                        "qualifier": "Default",
                        "autoHide": true,
                        "smartVariantManagement": false,
                        "tableSettings": {
                            "multiSelect": false,
                            "type": "AnalyticalTable"
                        },
                        "keyPerformanceIndicators": {
                            "kpiNayak": {
                                "model": "KPI1",
                                "entitySet": "Ztest_ALP_Report",
                                "qualifier": "KPIAMT"
                            }
                        }
                    }
                },
                "pages": {
                    "ObjectPage|Ztest_ALP_Report": {
                        "entitySet": "Ztest_ALP_Report",
                        "defaultLayoutTypeIfExternalNavigation": "MidColumnFullScreen",
                        "component": {
                            "name": "sap.suite.ui.generic.template.ObjectPage"
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    },
    "sap.fiori": {
        "registrationIds": [],
        "archeType": "analytical"
    }
}

 

Conclusion : In conclusion, KPIs in SAP Fiori Elements provide a quick and effective way to monitor critical business metrics. By presenting key values in a visual and easy-to-understand format, KPIs help users track performance and take timely actions to improve business outcomes.

Thank You,

B Naresh Kumar Nayak

entry.title: How to Create an Analytical List Page in SAP Fiori Elements – Step-by-Step Guide
entry.content:
Introduction : The Analytical List Page (ALP) in SAP Fiori Elements is a powerful floorplan designed for data analysis and monitoring business performance. It combines visual charts, visual filters, and a data table within a single page, allowing users to analyze large amounts of data efficiently. The Analytical List Page enables users to quickly identify trends, filter information, and drill down into detailed data for better business insights.

Analytical List Page (ALP) in SAP Fiori Elements:

The Analytical List Page (ALP) is a SAP Fiori Elements floorplan designed for data analysis and reporting. It combines charts, filters, and tables in a single page so users can analyze business data interactively.

ALP allows users to visualize, filter, and drill down into data without creating custom UI code.

Key Features of Analytical List Page:

  1.  Visual Filter--->The filter bar allows users to filter data using graphical representations.
  2. Analytical Charts--->Charts provide a visual representation of aggregated business data.
  3. Data Table--->The table shows detailed records corresponding to the chart data.
  4. Interactive Filtering--->When a user clicks a chart segment, the table automatically filters the related records.

Flow of  Analytical List Page:

  1. CDS View
  2. Annotation
  3. Odata service
  4. SAP Fiori Elements ( Analytical List Page) Template
  5. SAP Fiori Application UI

Step 1: Create CDS View and Metadata Extension

First, create the Core Data Services (CDS) view that exposes the required business data.
After creating the CDS view, create a Metadata Extension and add the necessary UI annotations to define how the data should appear on the SAP Fiori Elements Analytical List Page.

The metadata extension is used to separate the UI annotations from the main CDS view, which helps maintain a cleaner and more structured development approach.

Note: It is assumed that you already know how to create a CDS View and a Metadata Extension in the ABAP environment. Therefore, this article does not cover the step-by-step creation process. Instead, the examples below directly demonstrate the CDS view and Metadata Extension with the required annotations used for the SAP Fiori Elements Object Page.

1. CDS View:

Code:

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZALPR'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'ALP Report'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
define view Ztest_ALP_Report
  as select from ztest_sales_tab
{
  key vbeln as SalesDocument,
      vkorg as SalesOrg,
      werks as Plant,
      vtweg as DistrChannel,
      @DefaultAggregation: #SUM
      netwr as Amount,
      waerk as Currency,
      audat as D_Date,
      case substring( audat, 5, 2 )
      when '01' then concat( 'JAN-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '02' then concat( 'FEB-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '03' then concat( 'MAR-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '04' then concat( 'APR-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '05' then concat( 'MAY-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '06' then concat( 'JUN-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '07' then concat( 'JUL-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '08' then concat( 'AUG-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '09' then concat( 'SEP-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '10' then concat( 'OCT-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '11' then concat( 'NOV-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '12' then concat( 'DEC-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )

      end   as Month_Year
}

2. Meta data Extension:

 

Code:

@Metadata.layer: #CORE

@UI.chart: [{
    qualifier: 'chartDefault',
    description: 'Purchase Data',
    chartType: #PIE,
    dimensions: ['Month_Year'],
    measures: ['Amount'],
    dimensionAttributes: [{ dimension: 'Month_Year',role: #SERIES }],
    measureAttributes: [{ measure: 'Amount', role: #AXIS_1 }]
    }
   ]

@UI.presentationVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'Default',
    visualizations: [{ type: #AS_CHART ,qualifier: 'chartDefault'}]
   
     
}]



annotate entity Ztest_ALP_Report with
{
  .lineItem: [{ position: 10 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 10 }]

  SalesDocument;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 20 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 20 }]
  SalesOrg;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 30 }]
  Plant;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 40 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 40 }]
  DistrChannel;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 50 }]
  .dataPoint.qualifier: 'Amount'
  Amount;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 60 }]
  Currency;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 70 }]
  D_Date;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 80 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 80 }]
  Month_Year;

}

 

Step 2: Consume the CDS View in an OData Service

Note: It is assumed that you already know how to consume a CDS View in an OData service. Therefore, this article does not cover the step-by-step process. Instead, the explanation below directly continues with the required configuration and examples.

Go to SEGW Tcode and open your Project and   Add Your CDS View.

After adding the CDS entity, the Entity Type, Entity Set, and related properties will be generated automatically in the project.

Step 3:  Create SAP Fiori Application using Analytical List Page Template

Follow the steps below:

Open BAS  and Click  on File  and Select as New Project from Template  Chose  SAP Fiori  Generator Template under the Template Select as Analytical List Page and  Provide  Required Details and Finish it.

After completing these steps, the system automatically generates the SAP Fiori Elements Analytical List Page  application.

 

Once all the above steps are completed and the configuration is finished, preview the application in SAP Business Application Studio. The application will load successfully, and you will be able to see the Analytical List Page with the data displayed in charts and tables.

In the CDS annotations, I have configured the chart type as a Pie Chart by default. For this reason, the data is displayed as a Pie Chart in the above screenshot. However, the chart type can be changed based on the client's business requirements.
For Your Reference: This annotation has already been added in the Metadata Extension View.

Depending on the client’s requirements, you can change the chart type in the Fiori Elements application to formats such as Pie Chart, Column Chart, Bar Chart, or Line Chart. Please see the screenshot below for reference.

 

 

Conclusion: In conclusion, the Analytical List Page in SAP Fiori Elements provides an effective way to analyze and monitor business data through interactive charts, visual filters, and tables. By combining data visualization with filtering capabilities, it helps users gain insights quickly and make informed decisions more efficiently.

 

Please go below Links:

 Visual Filter: https://community.sap.com/t5/abap-blog-posts/kpi-key-performance-indicator-a-simple-guide/ba-p/14348850 

KPI(Key Performance Indicator): //community.sap.com/t5/abap-blog-posts/visual-filters-in-sap-fiori-elements-a-complete-guide/ba-p/14348265 

 

Thank You,

B Naresh Kumar Nayak

entry.title: Re: Avoiding Double Counting of Yield/Scrap in Rework Sce...
entry.content:
Hi @okakoshow 

SAP Digital Manufacturing (DM) sends yield/scrap confirmations to SAP ERP/SAP S/4HANA whenever an SFC completes an operation that is marked as an ERP reporting step. DM also allows an SFC to be re-processed at an operation it has already completed. In such a case, completing the same ERP reporting step again will trigger an additional confirmation, and the integrated ERP system will accumulate those quantities as you describe. The Production Yield Confirmation message no longer carries a “final confirmation” flag, so ERP sets the operation to PCNF and continues to sum posted quantities; CNF is only set after the order is completed in DM. There is no standard netting or de-duplication mechanism described that would automatically prevent double counting when the same reporting step is confirmed twice.

This is the recommended approach to avoid double counting.

  • Use a single ERP reporting step at the end of your routings:

    • The documentation recommends marking the last routing operation as the ERP reporting step to ensure correct reporting of consumed components and confirmations. With this setup, yield is only communicated when the SFC completes the final (reporting) operation, so sending the SFC back to earlier operations for rework will not trigger additional yield postings.
    • For planned orders, yield confirmation is explicitly sent after the completion of the last operation; aligning production orders with this pattern helps avoid duplicates.

  • Design rework as separate, non-reporting steps:

    • DM supports rework loops (for example TEST/DEBUG/REPAIR) within the main routing. Configure these rework operations without the ERP reporting flag so that rework does not generate additional yield confirmations.
    • If an SFC is scrapped on a non-reporting step, DM will automatically find a future reporting step in the routing and use it to send the scrap confirmation. This ensures scrap is reported once without relying on early, repeatable reporting steps.

Hope this helps.

Best,

Mike

entry.title: ProcessDirect Header Propagation Issue – BP_NUMBER Not Available in Called iFlows
entry.content:
Hello SAP Cloud Integration experts,

I’m working on an integration scenario where I’ve decomposed a large iFlow into multiple smaller iFlows using the ProcessDirect adapter. The main iFlow calls three separate iFlows in parallel:

Billing
BUPA
Accounting

Requirement

I need to pass a bp_number (Business Partner Number) from the incoming HTTP request to all called iFlows dynamically.

What I implemented

Extracted bp_number from query parameters using a Groovy script

Stored it as:
Exchange Property → BP_NUMBER
Message Header → BP_NUMBER

Before calling ProcessDirect, I ensured:

Header: BP_NUMBER = ${property.BP_NUMBER}

In the called iFlows, I use:

${header.BP_NUMBER}

inside HTTP receiver queries.

Issue

In all called iFlows, BP_NUMBER is coming as empty (''), causing API failures:

Billing API → 400 (Invalid token)
BUPA API → 404 (Empty key)
Accounting API → Empty filter

Example queries becoming:

SoldToParty eq ''
A_BusinessPartner('')
Customer eq ''

Observations

From MPL logs:
Header BP_NUMBER is correctly set in the main iFlow
But it is not available in child iFlows

I also noticed a removeHeaders[*] step in logs, though I have not explicitly added it.

Questions

Does removeHeaders[*] remove custom headers before ProcessDirect calls?
What is the best practice to ensure header propagation in ProcessDirect scenarios?
Do headers need to be explicitly allowed/configured for ProcessDirect?
Is using headers the recommended way to pass dynamic values across iFlows?

Closing

Any guidance or best practices would be greatly appreciated.
Thanks in advance!

entry.content:
Week 3: Challenge Completed. Package ZAC_16246

Auto generated code:

METHOD ValidateCategory.

    READ ENTITIES OF zr_personalexpensetracker IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY PersonalExpenseTracker
        FIELDS ( PaymentMethod )
        WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
      RESULT DATA(entities).

    LOOP AT entities INTO DATA(entity).
      " Validate if PaymentMethod is not within the allowed values
      IF entity-PaymentMethod <> 'EMI' AND
         entity-PaymentMethod <> 'CARD' AND
         entity-PaymentMethod <> 'CASH'.
        APPEND VALUE #( %tky = entity-%tky ) TO failed-PersonalExpenseTracker.
        APPEND VALUE #(
            %tky                = entity-%tky
            %state_area         = 'PaymentMethod'
            %msg                = new_message_with_text(
                                    text     = 'Invalid Payment Method. Allowed values are EMI, CARD, CASH.'
                                    severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error
                                  )
        ) TO reported-PersonalExpenseTracker.
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.

entry.title: Re: CBC behavior when an activated scope item is deprecat...
entry.content:
Hello @UmenoYagi,

the following two links can be helpful regarding scope item deactivation:

  • 3649879 - Deprecated Scope Items - SAP Central Business Configuration - SAP for Me
  • Deprecated Scope Items | SAP Help Portal

 

To apply the info from both links to your questions:

 

  • Will the successor scope item be automatically activated during the upgrade? 

    → No, SAP specifically recomends "to opt for the successor scope items (when available)" in the above mentioned KBA. Therefore, activation of the successor scope item is a manual task by the customer or implementation team.

 

  • Will it be automatically applied or transportedto the customer systems (e.g. test / production)?

    → No, a manual transport is necessary. After saving the scope change and deploying the scope and organizational structure in CBC a transport will be created that needs to be transported manually.

 

  • What happens to the original scope item that is deprecated or removed?
    • Is it automatically deactivated or deleted during the upgrade?

      → Here i can not give you a specific answer but an example from our set-up: Scope Item 1QR was deprecated with Release 2602:

      During our 2602 preparations we performed a check and left some notes. As of today, 1QR still appears in CBC under "My Selected Scope". So it seems that is has not been removed yet. 

       

I hope this helps.

Best regards,

Kerisha

entry.title: Re: How to Prepare an Indirect Method Cash Flow Statement...
entry.content:
Good day @JUNJUNPAN 

Thank you for your question.

What you described points to the app selection rather than to a simple data issue.

The generic Cash Flow Statement-Indirect Method app follows a predefined semantic model for FSV. In other words, you cannot freely derive any custom FSV structure you create. See KBA 2921199 for more details.

Because of that, the fact that you select your own FSV in Cash Flow Statement-Indirect Method does not mean that the measure columns will automatically follow the exact line logic of your Chinese Accounting Standards indirect cash flow layout. If you have not assigned the semantic tags, or if they do not match the app’s expected model, your report output will not align with your FSV structure in the way you expect. See KBA 2921199 for more insights.

The fact that the semantic tags shown in the learning content do not comply with Chines Accounting Standards is a strong sign that the generic app is not the right primary reporting tool for your CAS requirement. The reason is, it is not country specific and it differs from that semantic structure.

So, for your CAS cash flow statement, I recommend you use Cash Flow Statement-China for your localised solution. See KBA 3711550 and 3296727 for more details.

Consult these references for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/147436affce24e81892eee2076a78116/c8f7c396b89d480197500fa65eacaa8a.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/147436affce24e81892eee2076a78116/c8f7c396b89d480197500fa65eacaa8a.html?locale=en-US

To conclude, the issue is not that your FSV is wrong. The issue is that you are trying to use the wrong report framework for a China statutory requirement.

Here is a quick guide:

Open Cash Flow Statements-China app (note the system will prompt you to switch to the new version which is the “ Run Statutory Reports”)

Old version

New version

You need to use SSCUI 102805 Define Cash Flow Statement Version for China for this report. See KBA 3296727 for more insights.

In your configuration, make sure to review:

  1. The row structure of the China cash flow statement version
  2. The G/L account assignments per row
  3. The additional parameters per row
  4. The handling of invoice account, cash account and offsetting account where you deem relevant.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Fill Authorization Group Field while creating BP
entry.content:
Hi All,

While creating a new BP, I have a requirement where I need to auto-populate the Authorization Group field based on the Grouping field as shown below:

My requirement is when the Grouping is selected as REUS.

i.e., if Grouping = REUS → Authorization Group = REUS

I checked for relevant BADIs but couldn’t find a suitable one for this requirement. So, I’m trying to achieve this using BDT events.

I implemented my logic in event DSAVB (via TCode BUPT). Below is my code:

FUNCTION zri_bp_auth_group_fill.
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*"*"Local Interface:
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------

DATA: ls_but000 TYPE but000.

" Read BP general data from local memory
CALL FUNCTION 'BUP_BUPA_BUT000_GET'
IMPORTING
e_but000 = ls_but000.

IF ls_but000-bu_group = 'REUS'.

AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'B_BUPA_GRP'
ID 'BEGRU' FIELD 'REUS'
ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '01'.

IF sy-subrc = 0.

ls_but000-augrp = 'REUS'.

" Update the data back to memory
CALL FUNCTION 'BUP_BUPA_BUT000_COLLECT'
EXPORTING
i_subname = 'BUT000'
i_but000 = ls_but000.

ELSE.
MESSAGE e003(ZBP).
RETURN.
ENDIF.

ENDIF.

ENDFUNCTION.

The logic works when Grouping is REUS. But the issue is:

  • However, if I change the Grouping to a different value after selecting REUS, the system still reads it as REUS. Due to this, the Authorization Group field continues to be populated with REUS.
  • It looks like the buffer is not getting updated with the latest screen value when this event is triggered

So my questions are:

 

  • Is DSAVB the correct BDT event for this requirement?
  • At which BDT event can I access the latest updated value of BP Grouping from the screen buffer?
  • Or is there a better approach to handle this requirement?

Any suggestions would be really helpful.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: SAP on IBM i Webcast: Inject More Security into your IBM i SAP servers
entry.content:
Both SAP and IBM are closely monitoring all available sources of information to warn users of their software about newly discovered vulnerabilities and provide fixes as soon as possible. However, systems are not only at risk because of programming flaws. Incorrect security settings and careless granted authorities can also impact the security of systems negatively.

In the next SAP on IBM i webcast, IBM Senior Technical Staff Member and Db2 for i architect Scott Forstie will show how to use Db2 for IBM i SQL services to quickly identify potential security vulnerabilities. Example queries to examine user, object and command authorities for concerns are explored. Additionally, there are examples of how to check auditing settings of key commands that an attacker might use and whether your library lists are exposed. Christian Bartels, who works in the SAP on IBM i development team, will then comment on some of the typical findings for an SAP environment.

The webcast is scheduled for April 8th, 2026, at 4 pm CEST, 10 am EDT, 7:30 pm IST. Please visit the website at ibm.biz/SAP_on_IBM_i_Webcast to register for the webcast. In reply, you will receive a calendar entry to your email address with all the information that you need to join the webcast.

As usual, questions are welcome during the session. Some general Q&A time is also reserved at the end of the webcast for general questions and for your suggestions about possible topics for future webcasts.

If you can’t make the live session, a replay will be made available for registrants.

We hope to see you there!

entry.title: Re: AI-Augmented Oil Price Scenario Modelling in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hi AmanG,

thanks for trying out the widget and for sharing your findings!

I have to be transparent here: my implementation works because Perplexity's API explicitly allows cross-origin requests (Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *). SAP AI Core almost certainly does not — and from a security perspective, that makes total sense.

The core issue is a fundamental browser constraint: CORS is enforced server-side, and there is simply no way to work around it from the client/widget side. A very similar case was actually documented in the SAP Community for SAC Custom Widget → SAP CPI, where the official answer was: "This is expected behavior."

As for solutions — I honestly have to be careful here. BTP Destinations work well for UI5/Fiori apps, but I am not sure they are directly accessible from a SAC Custom Widget in the same way. So I suspect the realistic options are:

- A lightweight BTP Cloud Foundry proxy app (I know you'd prefer to avoid this)
- SAP API Management in front of AI Core (your "last resort" — but possibly the most robust SAP-native path)

I'd love to hear if anyone in the community has found a cleaner solution for this specific SAC → AI Core scenario!

Best,
Dirk

entry.title: Re: ABAP Performance Tuning in S/4HANA Series : Part 2 : The ST05 Playbook
entry.content:
Hi,

as said by @albatux : FOR ALL ENTRIES (FAE) can be optimized with sub-selects or joins like in his example or like below, where we join the db table vbap with the internal table lt_orders.

IF lt_orders IS NOT INITIAL.
  SELECT vbap~vbeln, vbap~posnr, vbap~matnr, vbap~kwmeng
    FROM vbap join @LT_orders as key on vbap~vbeln = key~vbeln
    INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_items).
ENDIF.

For larger internal tables (>100 rows) this pays off because  with FAE there are more roundtrips due to blocking 
done in the DBI (database interface) for FAE statements. Joins or Sub-Selects can use an optimized protocol where
no blocking takes place.

Btw. FAE can make use of this protocol (FDA, Fast Data Access) as well if the system is setup accordingly:
you can find more details here: 2399993 - FAQ: SAP HANA Fast Data Access (FDA) - SAP for Me
There is as well a DBI hint available to activate FDA on statement level for FAE:

    %_hints hdb '&PREFER_JOIN_WITH_FDA 1&'.

With FDA active for FAE there is no big difference any more between FAEs, sub-selects or joins.

Kind regards,

Hermann

entry.title: Requirement is to create multiple inspection lots for same batch/product with partial GR
entry.content:
Our requirement is to create multiple inspection lots when we receive partial GR for same Purchase Order/Batch/Product. Based on SAP standard settings system is combining all quantities to the inspection lot rather than creating a new one.

Example

PO - 10 pcs

First GR - 3 Pcs with Batch number BATCH123. A QI lot is created automatically with 3 PCS

Second GR is performed with 7 Pcs for same BATCH123 - Expectation is system should create a new QI lot. Rather it updates the first QI.

We are using S4HC Public Cloud 2SL landscape

entry.title: Tax Posting Issue with Sales Tax Payable G/L Account in Concur Integration
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

We are facing an issue while posting an invoice from SAP Concur using the G/L account 22002000 (Sales Tax Payable – CO). 

To investigate the problem, we tried reproducing the issue manually in the foreground using the Create Supplier Invoice app. During this process, we encountered the following two issues:

  1. When the tax G/L line item (account 22002000) was entered, the system displayed the error:
    “Please enter a value for field Base Amount for account 22002000.”

  2. To resolve this, we entered a base amount for the tax G/L item. However, after doing so, the system raised another error:
    “Tax entry not possible in this item.”

Could you please review this issue and advise on how it can be resolved? For reference, I have attached screenshots of both error messages.

Note: The G/L account 22002000 is configured with “Posting without tax allowed” = Yes.

Looking forward to your guidance.

Thanks

Naveen Kumar

entry.title: Leading zeros in CDS parameter lost during INA request processing in Analytical Query
entry.content:
In an Analytical Projection View (#ANALYTICAL_QUERY), I have a parameter of type NUMC(15).
When I send a parameter like 000000000000001 via an INA Service GetResponse request, the zeros are stripped and the query does not return results (and I need to send it with zeros)
I cannot use expressions like LPAD() in the projection parameter, and changing the parameter to CHAR15 does not solve the problem.
How can I ensure that the INA request correctly handles leading zeros so the projection view returns results from the database?

Here is the part of the body in the GetResponse request where I send the parameters:
"Variables": [
{
"Name": "!CDS_P_***",
"Values": {
"Selection": {
"SetOperand": {
"Elements": [
{
"key": 3285,
"Low": "000000000000001",
"IsExcluding": false,
"LowSupplements": [
"Version 1"
],
"Description": "***",
"tokenText": "***"
}
],
"FieldName": "***"
}
}
}
}
]

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Code – Technical Series – Feature Flags Service
entry.content:
Hi @MMonis 

Thank you for the question! Let me address each aspect:

CAP provides a built-in feature toggle system documented here: https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/extensibility/feature-toggles#feature-vector-providers

One important note on the documentation: it states "No features toggling for production yet... this sample is only for demonstration purposes. As user and tenant management is outside of CAP's scope, there's no out-of-the-box feature toggles provider for production yet." However, this does not mean the feature cannot be used in production. It simply means that the logic for determining which features are active for a given request is your responsibility, CAP provides the mechanism, but not the decision. That decision is exactly what a Feature Flag Service (e.g., SAP BTP Feature Flag Service) is designed to make.

I validated this with a small PoC and following the guide of CAP shared aboce. The setup is straightforward:

1. Features are defined as CDS extensions under a fts/ folder one subfolder per feature. Each feature can extend the data model with new fields and add Fiori annotations to control UI visibility.
2. A minimal Express middleware in server.js intercepts every incoming request, calls the Feature Flag Service, and sets req.features accordingly before CAP processes the request.
3. CAP's ModelProviderService then serves a feature-aware OData metadata document for that specific request.

The result is that UI elements (columns, fields, facets) appear or disappear automatically without any frontend code changes, because the Fiori UI reads $metadata on startup and reacts to what the model exposes. The same resolved feature list is also available in custom handlers via cds.context.features, so business logic can branch on it without making a second call to the flag service:

// Middleware resolves once per request
cds.on('bootstrap', app => app.use(async (req, res, next) => {
const userId = req.user?.id
const response = await featureFlagService.evaluate('loyalty-discount', { identifier: userId })
req.features = response.variation === 'true' ? ['loyalty-discount'] : []
next()
}))
// Handler consumes without a second round-trip
this.on('checkout', async (req) => {
if ('loyalty-discount' in cds.context.features) {
// call Loyalty Service, apply discount...
}
})

Regarding BPA, it depends on where you want to place the integration:

- If BPA is being triggered from within your CAP service (e.g., starting an automation process as part of a handler), implementing the feature flag check in the CAP middleware or handler makes the most sense. The flag controls whether the BPA call is even attempted, same pattern as the Loyalty Service example above.
- If BPA calling the CAP service, Since CAP exposes standard OData endpoints, BPA can consume them directly. In this case, it is theoretically possible to implement the feature flag logic on the BPA side, for example, conditionally executing process steps based on a flag evaluation before or after the OData call. I haven't tested this specific scenario, but given that BPA supports conditional branching and can make HTTP calls to external services, I expect to be feasible.

If you are building or consuming a multitenant application, there are some important things to be aware of, like:

- The team developing the multitenant application is responsible for defining and maintaining the features what each feature contains, which CDS extensions it includes, and how it affects the model.
- Feature assignment per tenant is managed at the infrastructure/configuration level.
- If your setup allows users to be identified per tenant, then it is entirely possible to resolve feature flags at the user or tenant level and feed them into CAP's model toggle system. Call Feature Flag Service with both userId and tenantId as identifiers before CAP processes anything.

I am a bit overloaded, but I am thinking about doing a second part of this blog from this question.

Kind regards,

Nata.

entry.title: Accelerating SAP ICSM and IRT integration with pre-packaged sample content
entry.content:
Introduction

Integrating SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management (ICSM) with IRT/RTSM systems is essential for running efficient clinical trials but many teams still end up reinventing the same integration patterns.

To help the ecosystem move faster, sample integration content for ICSM is now available on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, providing a practical starting point for building integrations between ICSM and IRT systems using SAP Integration Suite.

This post introduces the content, highlights the supported ICSM-IRT scenarios and explains how partners and customers can reuse and extend these building blocks to accelerate real‑world integrations.

Business value

Instead of starting from scratch, partners and customer can now:

  • Reuse proven integration patterns
  • Accelerate ICSM-IRT implementations
  • Focus on value‑adding extensions rather than plumbing
  • Align with SAP‑recommended architecture and standards

The content is intentionally modular, making it easier to adapt to different IRT systems or extend with additional scenarios.

What's Included

The published package SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management-Sample Integration with IRT demonstrates end-to-end integration scenarios across both planning and operations, based on GS1 Clinical Trials standards (3.6) and SAP-recommended integration patterns.

How to Consume the Accelerator Content

This can be consumed using SAP’s standard process for pre-packaged integration content: The pre-packaged integration content in SAP integration Suite is a set of Integration flows (iflows) leveraging the capabilities of SAP Integration Suite and SAP BTP to integrate IRT and SAP ICSM smoothly. This prepackage content takes care of many aspects of integration; some of these are as follows:

  1. Connect the IRT system to SAP Integration Suite artifacts via API management.
  2. Transformation of message payload from GS1 to ICSM and vice versa.
  3. Seamless integration of SAP Event Mesh to take an action against the events raised from ICSM (e.g. sales order created; Inventory report executed etc.).

This pre-package integration content supports the following interfaces for planning and operation in ICSM:

Interfaces for ICSM Planning

  1. Study Information
  2. IRT actuals and Master

Interfaces for ICSM Operations

  1. Inventory Report
  2. Shipment request and confirmation
  3. Dispatch Advice
  4. Receiving Advice
  5. Dispensation Advice
  6. Kit Status Change (Inbound/Outbound)

Below are the steps to consume the pre-packaged integration content:

Step 1 - Discover the package

Navigate to your SAP Integration Suite - Integration - Discover tab and search for the package "SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management Integration with IRT"

Below is the diagram shows the navigation steps.

                                                                                                              Fig: Integration Suite

Select the package and click copy to move it into your design workspace.  

Note: As with all standard packages, once you copy it, modifications in your workplace do not auto-inherit future SAP updates- SAP recommends keeping custom changes separate.

Step 2 - Review the Artifacts

Review each artifact integration flows and configure the user credentials for ICSM APIs and OAuth2 Client Credentials for event mesh using its service key values.

Step 3 - Configure the defined externalized parameters in each artifact

Update the values in each of the defined externalized parameters in each of the artifact integration flows.

The integration flows combine API-based and Event-driven approaches using SAP Integration Suite, SAP API Management, SAP Event Mesh and Cloud Connector, reflecting a reference architecture suitable for real-world landscapes.

Reference, not a Black Box

This is sample/accelerator content. Its purpose is to illustrate integration approaches and provide reusable building blocks - not to replace product features.

As with other SAP community assets, it does not come with the same lifecycle guarantees or support rigor as standard SAP product functionality. Instead, it is meant to enable the ecosystem to innovate, customize, and deliver differentiated solutions on top of SAP ICSM.

Get Started

  • Explore the sample Integration Package on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.
  • Review the ICSM-IRT Integration architecture and patterns.
  • Copy, adapt and extend the content for your own scenarios.

If you are an IRT vendor, Partner or Customer working with ICSM, then this is an opportunity to reduce the integration effort and contribute to a growing, interoperable ecosystem around clinical supply management.

This Accelerator Content lays out the groundwork for real-world ICSM-IRT integrations by providing pre-built, GS1 aligned, event driven flows that can be directly adopted for the proof-of-concept work. 

In the upcoming blog of this series, this accelerator content forms the technical backbone for validating the end-to-end data exchange between ICSM and an IRT platform, which accelerates onboarding IRT partners and helps to reduce the custom development effort.

Engage with us

If you are interested in learning more about SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management, please consider following the new "SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management".

We are happy to receive your feedback, questions or thoughts about SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management in a comment.

entry.title: Equivalence between classic extractors and CDS views
entry.content:
Hi all.
Why SAP hasn't publish a Note, document, whatever, that explain clear and concisely an equivalence between classic BW extractors and CDS views?
It´s a complete nosense to search through thousands of pages (notes, posts...), one by one extractor, if there is a CDS View that offers the same.
For example, consider 2LIS_02_ITM: SAP Note 2500202 tell us the extractor is "Whitelisted; DS working - no restrictions" (and no more information). However, we can find the C_PurchaseOrderItemDEX CDS View, that is "available for all external consumers who want to extract the data to SAP Business Warehouse (SAP BW) or other external systems.". So, can we replace 2LIS_02_ITM with C_PurchaseOrderItemDEX or not? Should we check field by field, join by join? What about a migration with cents of extractors?

Customers have invested tones of time and money developing and enhancing their ECC and BW sytems and deserves that.

entry.title: Service Catalog - Long text mass upload (ML01/ML02/ML03 Editor)
entry.content:
Upload Long Text for Service Catalog (ML01/ML02/ML03) using ABAP – Complete Guide with Working Solution

 Introduction

Uploading Long Text (Lg Text) for Service Catalog in SAP (Transactions ML01 / ML02 / ML03) is not straightforward.

Even if you use SAVE_TEXT, you may face issues like:

  • Only the first line gets updated

  • Remaining lines are ignored

  • Text appears incorrectly in ML03

  • No clear error message

👉 The root cause is not your code, but a hidden SAP design detail that is rarely documented.

This blog explains everything end-to-end:

  • Data model

  • Common mistakes

  • Root cause analysis

  • Fully working ABAP solution

⚠️ Prerequisite

Before uploading Long Text, ensure:

✔ Service Catalog already exists
✔ Service lines are present in the system
✔ Entries are available in table ESLZ

🔍 Why this is mandatory?

Long Text is linked with:

  • Service Type (LBNUM)

  • Edition (AUSGB)

  • Line (ZEILE)

👉 If ZEILE does not exist in ESLZ, the text:

  • Will not be visible in ML03

  • May get stored but not linked

🧠 Understanding the Data Model

1️⃣ ESLZ Table (Structure Only)

  • Stores service lines

  • Contains hierarchy (Block, Sub-lines)

  • DOES NOT store long text

2️⃣ Long Text Storage

Long Text is stored using SAPscript:

Table Purpose

STXHText Header
STXLText Lines

🔥 Text Key Mapping

Field Value

TDOBJECTESLZ
TDIDLTXT
TDNAMELBNUM + AUSGB + ZEILE

📊 Example Scenario

Excel Input

Service Edition Line Block TDLINE

001200400000000040000000004Test abc 4
001200400000000050000000004Test abc 5
001200400000000060000000004Test abc 6

Expected Output in ML03

Line 4 → Test abc 4  
Line 5 → Test abc 5  
Line 6 → Test abc 6

❌ Common Mistake (Root Cause)

Many implementations use:

GROUP BY lbnumm ausgb blknr.

Why this fails?

  • BLKNR = Block level

  • SAP stores text at LINE level (ZEILE)

Result:

❌ Only first text visible
❌ Remaining lines ignored
❌ Incorrect mapping in ML03

💥 Root Cause Explained

SAP Service Catalog Long Text is maintained per LINE (ZEILE), not per BLOCK (BLKNR)

✅ Correct Approach

✔ Process data line-wise (ZEILE)
✔ Generate text key using ZEILE
✔ Avoid grouping by BLKNR

💻 Complete Working ABAP Code

Below is a complete ABAP report which program to upload Service catalog long text, using Excel file upload.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report ZPR_SERVICE_CAT_UPLOAD
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
REPORT zpr_service_cat_upload.


*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* TYPES
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_excel,
         lbnumm   TYPE eslz-lbnum,
         ausgb    TYPE eslz-ausgb,
         zeile    TYPE eslz-zeile, "added on 23rd march
         sortn    TYPE eslz-sortn, "added on 23rd march
         blknr    TYPE eslz-blknr,
         meins    TYPE eslz-meins, "added on 23rd march
         tdformat TYPE tline-tdformat,
         tdline   TYPE tline-tdline,
         seq      TYPE i,
       END OF ty_excel.

DATA: gt_excel TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_excel,
      gs_excel TYPE ty_excel.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* RESULT STRUCTURE
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_result,
         icon    TYPE icon_d,
         lbnumm  TYPE eslz-lbnum,
         status  TYPE char10,
         message TYPE string,
       END OF ty_result.

DATA: gt_result TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_result,
      gs_result TYPE ty_result.

CONSTANTS:
  gc_sheet_exclude TYPE string VALUE 'Sheet1'.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* SELECTION SCREEN
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
PARAMETERS: p_file TYPE string OBLIGATORY.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* F4 HELP
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR p_file.

  DATA: lt_filetab TYPE filetable,
        lv_rc      TYPE i.

  CALL METHOD cl_gui_frontend_services=>file_open_dialog
    CHANGING
      file_table = lt_filetab
      rc         = lv_rc.

  IF lv_rc > 0.
    READ TABLE lt_filetab INTO DATA(ls_file) INDEX 1.
    p_file = ls_file-filename.
  ENDIF.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* START-OF-SELECTION
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
START-OF-SELECTION.

  PERFORM read_excel.
  PERFORM upload_texts.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* READ EXCEL
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM read_excel.

  DATA: lo_excel         TYPE REF TO cl_fdt_xl_spreadsheet,
        lv_xstring       TYPE xstring,
        lt_raw           TYPE solix_tab,
        lv_len           TYPE i,
        lv_worksheetname TYPE string,
        lt_worksheets    TYPE string_table,
        lo_data          TYPE REF TO data.

* Upload file
  CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_UPLOAD'
    EXPORTING
      filename   = p_file
      filetype   = 'BIN'
    IMPORTING
      filelength = lv_len
    TABLES
      data_tab   = lt_raw.

  CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_BINARY_TO_XSTRING'
    EXPORTING
      input_length = lv_len
    IMPORTING
      buffer       = lv_xstring
    TABLES
      binary_tab   = lt_raw.

* Create Excel object
  TRY.
      CREATE OBJECT lo_excel
        EXPORTING
          document_name = p_file
          xdocument     = lv_xstring.
    CATCH cx_fdt_excel_core.
      MESSAGE 'Error reading Excel file' TYPE 'E'.
  ENDTRY.

  lo_excel->if_fdt_doc_spreadsheet~get_worksheet_names(
    IMPORTING worksheet_names = lt_worksheets ).

  DELETE lt_worksheets WHERE table_line = gc_sheet_exclude.

  READ TABLE lt_worksheets INTO lv_worksheetname INDEX 1.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    MESSAGE 'Worksheet not found' TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.

  lo_data = lo_excel->if_fdt_doc_spreadsheet~get_itab_from_worksheet(
               worksheet_name = lv_worksheetname ).

* Convert to internal table
  CALL FUNCTION 'Z_XLS_INTO_ITAB'
    EXPORTING
      header      = 'X'
      str         = 'ZTS_SC_EXCEL'
      iv_data_ref = lo_data
      hdr_len     = '1'
    IMPORTING
      it_xls_data = gt_excel.

  IF gt_excel IS INITIAL.
    MESSAGE 'No data found in Excel file' TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.

ENDFORM.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* UPLOAD TEXTS
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM upload_texts.

  DATA: lt_grouped TYPE SORTED TABLE OF ty_excel
                      WITH UNIQUE KEY lbnumm ausgb blknr,
        ls_group   TYPE ty_excel.


* Sort properly
  SORT gt_excel BY lbnumm ausgb blknr seq.

** Grouping loop (modern syntax)
*  SORT gt_excel BY lbnumm ausgb blknr seq.

"commented on 23.03

*  LOOP AT gt_excel INTO DATA(ls_excel)
*       GROUP BY ( lbnumm = ls_excel-lbnumm
*                  ausgb  = ls_excel-ausgb
*                  zeile  = ls_excel-zeile
*                  blknr  = ls_excel-blknr )
*       ASCENDING
*       ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<group>).
*
*    DATA: lt_lines       TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF tline,
*          lt_group_lines TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_excel,
*          ls_header      TYPE thead.
*
**--- Build NAME (with ALPHA)
*    DATA(lv_lbnumm) = |{ <group>-lbnumm ALPHA = IN }|.
*    DATA(lv_ausgb)  = <group>-ausgb.
*    DATA(lv_blknr)  = |{ <group>-blknr ALPHA = IN }|.
*
**    DATA(lv_name) = lv_lbnumm && lv_ausgb && lv_blknr.
*    DATA lv_name TYPE thead-tdname.
*
*    lv_name = lv_lbnumm && lv_ausgb && lv_blknr.
*
**--- Step 1: Move group data into internal table
*    lt_group_lines = VALUE #( FOR wa IN GROUP <group> ( wa ) ).
*
**--- Step 2: Sort by SEQ (VERY IMPORTANT)
*    SORT lt_group_lines BY seq.
*
**--- Step 3: Prepare ALL lines
*    CLEAR lt_lines.
*
*    LOOP AT lt_group_lines INTO DATA(ls_line_data).
*
*      IF ls_line_data-tdline IS NOT INITIAL.
*        APPEND VALUE tline(
*          tdformat = ls_line_data-tdformat                     " <-- FIX
*          tdline   = ls_line_data-tdline ) TO lt_lines.
*      ENDIF.
*
*    ENDLOOP.
*
**--- Header
*    ls_header = VALUE thead(
*        tdobject = 'ESLZ'
*        tdname   = lv_name
*        tdid     = 'LTXT'
*        tdspras  = sy-langu ).
*
*
*
**--- Save text (overwrite mode)
*    CALL FUNCTION 'SAVE_TEXT'
*      EXPORTING
*        header = ls_header
**       insert = space
*        insert = space
*       savemode_direct = 'X'
*      TABLES
*        lines  = lt_lines
*      EXCEPTIONS
*        OTHERS = 1.
*
**--- Result handling
*    DATA(ls_result) = VALUE ty_result(
*        lbnumm = <group>-lbnumm ).
*
*    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
*
*      DATA lv_msg TYPE string.
*
*      MESSAGE ID sy-msgid TYPE sy-msgty NUMBER sy-msgno
*        INTO lv_msg
*        WITH sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.
*
*      ls_result-status  = 'ERROR'.
*      ls_result-message = lv_msg.
*      ls_result-icon    = '@5C@'.
*
*    ELSE.
*
*
**      CALL FUNCTION 'COMMIT_TEXT'
**        EXPORTING
**          object           = ls_header-tdobject
**          name             = ls_header-tdname
**          id               = ls_header-tdid
**          language         = sy-langu
***         savemode_direct  = abap_true.
*          ls_result-status = 'SUCCESS'.
*      ls_result-message = 'Text Saved Successfully'.
*      ls_result-icon    = '@5B@'.
*
*    ENDIF.
*
*    APPEND ls_result TO gt_result.
*
*  ENDLOOP.
*
*  COMMIT WORK.

"commented on 23.03

  DATA: lt_lines  TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF tline,
        ls_header TYPE thead,
        ls_result TYPE ty_result.

  LOOP AT gt_excel INTO DATA(ls_excel).

*--- Build TEXT NAME using ZEILE (IMPORTANT FIX)
    DATA(lv_lbnumm) = |{ ls_excel-lbnumm ALPHA = IN }|.
    DATA(lv_ausgb)  = ls_excel-ausgb.
    DATA(lv_zeile)  = |{ ls_excel-zeile ALPHA = IN }|.

    DATA(lv_name) = lv_lbnumm && lv_ausgb && lv_zeile.

*--- Prepare text lines (single line per ZEILE)
    CLEAR lt_lines.

    IF ls_excel-tdline IS NOT INITIAL.
      APPEND VALUE tline(
        tdformat = ls_excel-tdformat
        tdline   = ls_excel-tdline ) TO lt_lines.
    ENDIF.

*--- Header
    ls_header = VALUE thead(
      tdobject = 'ESLZ'
      tdname   = lv_name
      tdid     = 'LTXT'
      tdspras  = sy-langu ).

*--- Clear result
    CLEAR ls_result.
    ls_result-lbnumm = ls_excel-lbnumm.

*--- SAVE TEXT
    CALL FUNCTION 'SAVE_TEXT'
      EXPORTING
        header          = ls_header
        insert          = space
        savemode_direct = 'X'
      TABLES
        lines           = lt_lines
      EXCEPTIONS
        OTHERS          = 1.

*--- Handle result
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.

      DATA: lv_msg TYPE string.

      MESSAGE ID sy-msgid TYPE sy-msgty NUMBER sy-msgno
        INTO lv_msg
        WITH sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.

      ls_result-status  = 'ERROR'.
      ls_result-message = lv_msg.
      ls_result-icon    = '@5C@'.

    ELSE.

      ls_result-status  = 'SUCCESS'.
      ls_result-message = |Text saved for line { ls_excel-zeile }|.
      ls_result-icon    = '@5B@'.

    ENDIF.

    APPEND ls_result TO gt_result.

  ENDLOOP.

*--- Final commit
  COMMIT WORK.

  PERFORM display_alv.


ENDFORM.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* DISPLAY ALV
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM display_alv.

  DATA: lt_fieldcat TYPE slis_t_fieldcat_alv,
        ls_fieldcat TYPE slis_fieldcat_alv,
        ls_layout   TYPE slis_layout_alv.

* ICON
  CLEAR ls_fieldcat.
  ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'ICON'.
  ls_fieldcat-icon      = 'X'.
  ls_fieldcat-seltext_m = 'Status'.
  APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

* LBNUM
  CLEAR ls_fieldcat.
  ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'LBNUMM'.
  ls_fieldcat-seltext_m = 'Package'.
  APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

* STATUS
  CLEAR ls_fieldcat.
  ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'STATUS'.
  ls_fieldcat-seltext_m = 'Status'.
  APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

* MESSAGE
  CLEAR ls_fieldcat.
  ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'MESSAGE'.
  ls_fieldcat-seltext_m = 'Message'.
  APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

  ls_layout-zebra = 'X'.

  CALL FUNCTION 'REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY'
    EXPORTING
      i_callback_program = sy-repid
      is_layout          = ls_layout
      it_fieldcat        = lt_fieldcat
    TABLES
      t_outtab           = gt_result.

ENDFORM.  


Function Module to read Excel sheet into internal table - 
FUNCTION z_xls_into_itab.
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*"*"Local Interface:
*"  IMPORTING
*"     REFERENCE(P_FILE) TYPE  RLGRAP-FILENAME OPTIONAL
*"     REFERENCE(HEADER) TYPE  CHAR1 DEFAULT 'X'
*"     REFERENCE(STR) TYPE  CHAR30 OPTIONAL
*"     REFERENCE(IV_DATA_REF) TYPE REF TO  DATA OPTIONAL
*"     VALUE(HDR_LEN) TYPE  SYST_TABIX OPTIONAL
*"  EXPORTING
*"     REFERENCE(IT_XLS_DATA) TYPE  STANDARD TABLE
*"  TABLES
*"      T_FILE STRUCTURE  ALSMEX_TABLINE OPTIONAL
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------

  TYPE-POOLS: abap,slis.
  FIELD-SYMBOLS:<dy_table> TYPE STANDARD TABLE,
   <fs_current_field>,
   <wa_str>,<wa_temp>
  .
  DATA:new_line      TYPE REF TO data,
       new_line2     TYPE REF TO data,
       li_fieldcat   TYPE lvc_t_fcat,
       dy_table      TYPE REF TO data,
* t_file      TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF alsmex_tabline,
       wa_file       TYPE alsmex_tabline,
       v_col         TYPE i,
       begin_row     TYPE i,
       zls_alsm_data TYPE zts_alsmex_tabline.

********This will provide Fields in Row format of Structure created(which should be similar to Excel Format).
  CALL FUNCTION 'LVC_FIELDCATALOG_MERGE'
    EXPORTING
      i_structure_name       = str
    CHANGING
      ct_fieldcat            = li_fieldcat
    EXCEPTIONS
      inconsistent_interface = 1
      program_error          = 2
      OTHERS                 = 3.

  IF sy-subrc EQ 0.

********This will create internal table which will have fields as per the Excel Format.
    CALL METHOD cl_alv_table_create=>create_dynamic_table
      EXPORTING
        it_fieldcatalog           = li_fieldcat
        i_length_in_byte          = 'X'
      IMPORTING
        ep_table                  = dy_table
      EXCEPTIONS
        generate_subpool_dir_full = 1
        OTHERS                    = 2.
    IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
      ASSIGN dy_table->* TO <dy_table>.
    ENDIF.

******V_col will hold the number of Columns inside Excel File which is later passed to ALSM_EXCEL_TO_INTERNAL_TABLE’ .

    DESCRIBE TABLE li_fieldcat LINES v_col.

    LOOP AT t_file INTO wa_file.

******This Block will create new area to store column wise data.<wa_temp> acts as a work area which is completely blank at start of new row
      AT NEW row.
        CREATE DATA new_line2 LIKE LINE OF <dy_table>.
        ASSIGN new_line2->* TO <wa_temp>.
      ENDAT.

*******for every iteration, wa_file-col will have increment till number of columns in Excel file and then reset to 1 when there’s new row i.e row = 1. (Refer example at end of blog)

      ASSIGN COMPONENT wa_file-col OF STRUCTURE <wa_temp> TO <fs_current_field>.
      IF sy-subrc EQ 0.

        <fs_current_field> = wa_file-value.
        UNASSIGN <fs_current_field>.
      ENDIF.

*****Finally once all Columns are processed and new row is about to start, append the workarea into Internal table

      AT END OF row.
        APPEND <wa_temp> TO it_xls_data.
        UNASSIGN <fs_current_field>.
        UNASSIGN <wa_temp>.
      ENDAT.

    ENDLOOP.
*ENDIF.

***Mapping IV_DATA_REF (Excel data) to Fields

*-----------------------FIELD SYMBOLS----------------------------------*
    FIELD-SYMBOLS: <lfs_data_tab> TYPE ANY TABLE,
                   <fs_1>         TYPE any,
                   <fs_2>         TYPE any.
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
    hdr_len = COND #( WHEN hdr_len IS INITIAL THEN 1 ELSE hdr_len ).

    ASSIGN iv_data_ref->* TO <lfs_data_tab>.

    IF <lfs_data_tab> IS ASSIGNED AND <dy_table> IS ASSIGNED.

      LOOP AT <lfs_data_tab> ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_data>).
        IF sy-tabix LE hdr_len.
          CONTINUE.  "Skip line 1
        ENDIF.

        CREATE DATA new_line2 LIKE LINE OF <dy_table>.
        ASSIGN new_line2->* TO <wa_temp>.

        DO 250 TIMES.
          ASSIGN COMPONENT sy-index OF STRUCTURE <lfs_data> TO <fs_1>.
          ASSIGN COMPONENT sy-index OF STRUCTURE <wa_temp> TO <fs_2>.
          READ TABLE li_fieldcat INTO DATA(fs_fieldcat) INDEX sy-index .
          IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL AND fs_fieldcat-datatype = 'DATS'.
            IF <fs_1> IS ASSIGNED AND <fs_2> IS ASSIGNED AND strlen( <fs_1> ) EQ 10 .
              DATA(lv_date) = |{ <fs_1>(4) }{ <fs_1>+5(2) }{ <fs_1>+8(2) }| .
              CONDENSE lv_date.
              <fs_2> = lv_date.
*              <fs_2> = <fs_1>.
              UNASSIGN: <fs_1>, <fs_2>.
              CLEAR: lv_date.
            ENDIF.
          ELSE.
            IF <fs_1> IS ASSIGNED AND <fs_2> IS ASSIGNED.
              <fs_2> = <fs_1>.
              UNASSIGN: <fs_1>, <fs_2>.
            ENDIF.
          ENDIF.
        ENDDO.

        IF <wa_temp> IS ASSIGNED AND <wa_temp> IS NOT INITIAL.
*          MOVE-CORRESPONDING <wa_temp> TO it_xls_data.
          APPEND <wa_temp> TO it_xls_data.
        ENDIF.

      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

ENDFUNCTION.

⚠️ Important Points to Remember

  • ❌ Do NOT use BLKNR for text key

  • ✅ Always use ZEILE

  • ❌ Avoid INSERT = 'X'

  • ✅ Use INSERT = SPACE for overwrite

  • ✅ Ensure ESLZ entries exist

🧪 Debug Tip

Use function module READ_TEXT:

OBJECT = 'ESLZ'
ID     = 'LTXT'
NAME   = '001200400000000004'

👉 Helps verify actual stored text

Final Result

✔ All lines updated correctly
✔ Correct mapping in ML03
✔ No missing or overwritten data

 Conclusion

Uploading Service Catalog Long Text becomes easy once you understand:

  • SAP stores text per LINE (ZEILE)

  • Not per BLOCK (BLKNR)

  • Correct text key is the key to success

💡 Key Takeaway

If your long text upload is not working — you are most likely using the wrong key.

🙌 Closing Note

This is a common issue with no clear documentation.
Hope this blog saves you hours of debugging.

Feel free to share your feedback or improvements 🚀

entry.title: Re: CALLING SAP HANA STORED PROCEDURES WITH VARIABLES
entry.content:
Hi,

Not 100% sure about the exact syntax for HANA, but try something like this:

/* select * from OCRD c */
/* select * from OITM i */
DECLARE CardCode NVARCHAR(50);
DECLARE ItemCode NVARCHAR(50);
CardCode := /* c.CardCode */ '[%0]';
ItemCode := /* i.ItemCode */ '[%1]';

CALL MY_PROC(CardCode, ItemCode);

Regards,

Johan

entry.title: Is it possible to connect two XI adapters to the same SAP backend (PI and Integration Suite)?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

we are currently evaluating a migration from SAP PI/PO to the SAP Integration Suite.

In our current setup, several interfaces use ABAP proxies via the XI adapter.

During the transition phase we are wondering:

  • Is it technically possible to connect two XI adapter endpoints to the same SAP backend system as sender and receiver?
    • one pointing to PI/PO
    • one pointing to Integration Suite

Questions:

  1. Can an SAP backend system maintain two XI adapter connections simultaneously?
  2. How would the proxy decide which endpoint to use (logical port, configuration, etc.)?
  3. Are there any best practices or limitations for this setup during migration?

Any experiences or recommendations would be highly appreciated.

Thanks
