entry.title: Re: SAP BusinessObjects BI platform taking considerable t...
entry.content: I'd probably recommend creating a case for this. There's no feasible reason that should take > 2 hours. It's probably the command that is supposed to be executed is stuck, we've seen similar issues in the past. Some of the commands can take a long time if they rely on a temporary CMS, for example AddNode and Deployfiles scripts start a temporary CMS and login. If there's an issue during the login there's a period of waiting where we are persistently trying to logon - but no waiting period I'm aware of that should take > 2 hours.

entry.title: Re: Questions About Display Options in the New SAP Succes...
entry.content:
Hi @Joe0603 

1. The category is only editable if you access the Edit Goal (Edit Target from your screenshot), but otherwise, it shows under the Goal Name in the side panel. The reasoning behind might be on just having the most common actions done on the form (rating, adding comments, creating an activity...) but for other attributes, like metric, category or weight, the goal should be opened on the edit mode, which can be easily done on the form.

As per your question "Is there a way to display the category in view mode in the new UI?", if you would like to enforce the category to be read-only even when editing the goal, this is possible with Goal Plan states. If you set up a restriction on the control-category-element field, you could make the category read-only or even not visible when the goal plan has a specific status. The change of goal plan state can be done in the Goal Plan itself or when the form moves to a specific step. 

2072109 - Configuring & Using Goal Plan State in SuccessFactors Goal Management https://me.sap.com/notes/2072109/E

Additionally, in 1H 2026 release there will be a new Mass Change Goal Plan States tool where administrators can update multiple goal plan statuses.

2. In Legacy Form experience, you could use this code below, and according to the KBA 3669517 - Cannot hide Rating Scale Label Link in Performance Form Summary section https://me.sap.com/notes/3669517/E  this should be also supported in Latest Experience next 1H 2026 release.  What I am not 100% if this would also be applicable to Goal and Attribute sections, since the icon on the Summary looks different, although they have the same purpose to display the rating scale levels and descriptions.

<field-permission type="none">
<role-name>*</role-name>
<field refid="rating-scale-label-link"></field>
<route-step stepid="*"/>
</field-permission>

3.  Are there field restrictions on the calculated overall rating? I wonder if you could display that rating on the calculated rating field and you can hide or even disable the manual overall rating. Does it show different results? 

Hope these will be helpful,

Thanks!

Jordi

entry.title: Artificial Intelligence in Organizational Change Management: From Theory to Practice
entry.content:
As a quick recap: in our previous post, we explored the impact of AI across our six

Organizational Change Management (OCM) dimensions:  In our previous post (read the article here), we explored HOW AI impacts the six OCM dimensions.

Now let's get practical: WHAT can you do with it - now?

Based on conversations with change professionals across industries, we've identified three high-impact opportunities where AI is already delivering results in SAP transformations.

Being in mutual exchange with other change professionals, we have identified many opportunities for the improvement of transformation & change projects by using AI capabilities to solve several challenges.

From streamlining content creation to enabling impactful communication on tight budgets and acting as a strategic sparring partner, AI opens new possibilities for tackling challenges and driving innovation in OCM. Below, you’ll find a graphic that presents the three opportunities at a glance.

 

 

 

 

 

1st Opportunity – Transforming Content Creation with AI

Scenario
Your teams produce great ideas—but turning workshop outcomes into on‑brand, stakeholder‑ready assets is slow, manual, inconsistent, and scattered across channels. Deadlines stack up, style guides get bent, and version control becomes a guessing game. You need a way to turn raw inputs into professional, audience‑specific materials without the heavy lifting.

AI Solution
Use generative AI to turn workshop outputs, briefs, and project artifacts into on‑brand deliverables: vision statements, key messages, stakeholder briefings, enablement outlines, and even first‑cut engagement plans. Configure brand and tone guardrails, map stakeholder preferences, and let AI adapt content by audience, channel, and language —reducing formatting work while improving consistency.

Real Impact
You can move from raw inputs to polished, customer‑specific content faster, keep materials aligned to your brand and narrative, and tailor messages to stakeholder needs with less manual effort. Content creation becomes a coordinated flow across change strategy, communication, and execution—supporting momentum throughout the program.

Getting Started
Collect a small set of canonical inputs (brand guidelines, message house, workshop notes, key slides) and define master prompts and templates for each asset type. Pilot with two priority deliverables—such as a vision statement and a stakeholder briefing—keep a human review step for accuracy and tone and expand to additional channels once the workflow proves reliable.

 

2nd Opportunity – Realizing appealing communication with tight budgets 

Scenario
The plan is solid, the milestones are set—but the budget for agencies and production support is too expensive. You still need a steady cadence of videos, posts, articles, and emails that land with different audiences and regions. The choice shouldn’t be “go quiet” or “burn out the team.”

AI Solution
Use generative AI to transform SAP standard content and your project artifacts (charters, release notes, FAQs, town‑hall transcripts) into ready‑to‑use assets—short explainer videos, community posts, newsletters, and stakeholder emails. Apply brand and tone guardrails, translate or reuse for key markets, and generate channel‑specific variants with approvals routed to your team.

Real Impact
You can maintain a consistent communications drumbeat without additional headcount, reach more audiences in their preferred channels and languages, and keep messages aligned to project milestones—preserving quality while staying within budget.

Getting Started
Identify your source content and priority channels, define brand/voice prompts and approval rules, and pilot two to three asset types (e.g., a monthly newsletter, a short video, and a stakeholder email). Keep human review for accuracy and context, then expand once the workflow feels reliable.

 

3rd Opportunity – Engage AI as your OCM sparring partner

Scenario
You’ve got strong change ideas, but not always the time—or the bench—to test them, iterate fast, and pressure‑test assumptions. Workshops end, momentum fades, and decisions wait for the next availability slot. You need a trusted counterpart that can challenge thinking and turn concepts into actionable plans on demand.

AI Solution
Set up an AI assistant grounded in your internal playbooks, past project lessons, and SAP good practices. Use it to iterate on change narratives, draft stakeholder maps and engagement plans, compare scenario options, summarize feedback patterns, and propose next‑best actions—available on demand, 24/7.

Real Impact
You can move from idea to plan faster, explore more options before committing, and document the rationale behind decisions. The assistant augments your expertise with rapid synthesis and structured recommendations, while you retain judgment and sign‑off.

Getting Started
Curate a safe, high‑quality knowledge base (frameworks, project docs, templates, lessons learned), define clear prompts and review criteria, and begin with co‑drafting tasks such as sponsor briefings or comms outlines. Validate outputs with your team, then extend to scenario testing and decision support as confidence grows.

Looking ahead

AI brings new momentum to how we deliver change—but its impact depends on how intentionally we choose to work with it. Start small with a clearly scoped use case, define success metrics, and iterate based on evidence. Keep experts in the loop throughout: AI accelerates first drafts, clustering, and signal detection, while humans ensure narrative, nuance, and sound decision‑making. Establish privacy, security, and responsible‑AI guardrails from day one to build trust and transparency.

But most importantly: don’t wait for “perfect AI.”
Experiment, learn, adapt—and share your experiences. Your insights help the wider change community move forward faster.

✓ Start with ONE high-pain use case (e.g., stakeholder analysis automation)

✓ Define success metrics upfront (time saved, quality scores, adoption lift)

✓ Keep humans in the loop: AI drafts, experts review and decide

✓ Establish guardrails: privacy, security, bias checks—non-negotiable from day one

We should not be waiting for perfect AI—keep experimenting, learning, and iterating.

If you want to go deeper or need support setting up your first AI‑powered OCM workflow, reach out via maja.polke@gmail.com or hannah.bruelke@gmail.com or explore our SharePoint site for templates, guides, and examples.

Together, let’s shape how AI elevates OCM—today and for the future.

I searched SWDC, but didn't found it...

entry.title: Re: Governing the Emerging Collective Intelligence
entry.content:
Thank you,  @sandiago, I really appreciate your thoughtful perspective. I especially agree with your point on the shift from managing data to governing intelligence — that feels like a fundamental transition we’re starting to see across enterprise systems. A strong data foundation is clearly essential, as you mentioned. It defines what the system considers “truth.” It will be interesting to see how this evolves — especially as customers begin combining their own data platforms, models, and agents with SAP’s applications. That’s where governance becomes not just a control mechanism, but a shared responsibility across the ecosystem.

I’m planning to continue exploring this topic further and go deeper into how governance needs to evolve in this context — really appreciate the inspiration your comment adds to that direction.

entry.title: Unlock Enterprise-Scale Integration and Automation with SAP: A 5-Part Webcast Series
entry.content:
Running integration at enterprise scale requires more than connecting systems - it demands a unified strategy, consistent governance, and a strong operational foundation. To help organizations navigate this complex landscape, we’re excited to announce a deep-dive 5-part webcast series that shows how SAP Integration Suite and SAP Business Technology Platform can serve as a true enterprise-wide integration platform.

Whether you’re looking to simplify complexity, strengthen governance, or enable future-ready automation, this series provides a structured path from vision to sustainable execution. Here’s a breakdown of what you can expect from each session:

 

1. Rethinking Integration: Building a Unified Enterprise-wide Platform

Integration starts with consolidation and unification. This session explores how organizations can position SAP Integration Suite as a central platform, connecting systems, processes, and data across the enterprise. Learn strategies to streamline your integration landscape and create a foundation for scalable, enterprise-wide operations.

Register here:  https://event.on24.com/eventRegistration/EventLobbyServlet?target=reg20.jsp&eventid=5256733&sessionid=1&key=2F2D713929E44EC97A0255F0674D0915&groupId=6661068&sourcepage=register

 

2. From Vision to Execution: Building a Scalable Integration Strategy

Turning integration vision into reality requires a deliberate, structured approach. This session dives into the steps organizations can take to develop a scalable integration strategy that aligns business goals with technology execution. Discover best practices for governance, architecture, and execution that drive measurable value.

Register here: https://event.on24.com/eventRegistration/EventLobbyServlet?target=reg20.jsp&eventid=5256733&sessionid=1&key=2F2D713929E44EC97A0255F0674D0915&groupId=6661111&sourcepage=register

 

3. Achieve Enterprise-grade Operations for SAP Integration Suite

Operational excellence is critical to sustaining integration at scale. In this session, we focus on how to strengthen governance, monitor performance, and build resilient processes with SAP Integration Suite. Learn how enterprise-grade operations ensure consistency, reliability, and scalability across your integration landscape.

Regiser here: https://event.on24.com/eventRegistration/EventLobbyServlet?target=reg20.jsp&eventid=5259511&sessionid=1&key=905F0343E0BA503A283C97B3645BCB31&groupId=6667225&sourcepage=register

 

4. Drive Intelligent Business Transformation with Agentic Enterprise Automation

Intelligent business transformation requires more than isolated automation - it demands connected, adaptive processes powered by AI. Explore how SAP Business Technology Platform and SAP Business Transformation Management enable agentic enterprise automation at scale. Learn how to design, govern, and measure AI agents that drive true process transformation, generating measurable outcomes across complex enterprise environments.

Register here: https://event.on24.com/eventRegistration/EventLobbyServlet?target=reg20.jsp&eventid=5259684&sessionid=1&key=10935CA2537B96624DD065F7EA514C2F&groupId=6661382&sourcepage=register

 

5. Develop Robust Automations with SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite

Agentic enterprise automation only succeeds when systems, workflows, and applications are seamlessly connected. This session demonstrates how SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite work together to enable end-to-end automation across SAP and non-SAP landscapes. See live demos, customer success stories, and practical examples showing how low-code development and enterprise-grade integration accelerate automation while maintaining control, visibility, and scalability.

Register here: https://event.on24.com/eventRegistration/EventLobbyServlet?target=reg20.jsp&eventid=5258498&sessionid=1&key=76AD4CD903C206A20A2CFFE8270CC25C&groupId=6661286&sourcepage=register

 

This series is designed for integration architects, IT leaders, and business transformation professionals who want to take their enterprise integration and automation initiatives to the next level. Join us to learn how SAP can help you simplify complexity, achieve operational excellence, and unlock intelligent, future-ready automation.

Save your spot 🙂

entry.title: Administering Sustainability Control Tower
entry.content:
Why Administration Matters

Before organizations can effectively manage sustainability data, they need a solid technical foundation. SAP Sustainability Control Tower requires proper setup, role management, and system integration to ensure accurate, consistent, and scalable operations.

A well-administered system enables:

  • Reliable data integration across SAP landscapes
  • Secure and structured user access
  • Smooth operation for business users
  • A strong foundation for reporting and steering sustainability

What You’ll Learn in This Learning Journey

This compact, 1.5-hour course focuses on the essential administrative tasks required to get SAP Sustainability Control Tower up and running:

  1. Setting Up Accounts and Services
    Learn how to manage accounts and services to prepare the system for use.
  2. Assigning Roles
    Understand different user roles and how to assign them to ensure proper access control and governance.
  3. Configuring System Integration
    Connect SAP Sustainability Control Tower with key systems, including:
  • SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • SAP Datasphere
  • SAP EHS Management
  • SAP Sustainability Footprint Management
  • SAP Analytics Cloud

This ensures seamless data exchange and a fully connected sustainability landscape.

Prerequisite: Basic knowledge from the Learning Journey Reporting and Steering Sustainability in Your Businesses using SAP Sustainability Control Tower.

Who Should Take the Course?

This learning journey is designed for:

  • System Administrators responsible for setup and configuration

Build the technical foundation for effective sustainability management and start the Learning Journey “Administering SAP Sustainability Control Tower” here.
